---
title: "Phase 2: Pharmacogenomic Genotyping"
description: "CYP2D6/CYP2C19 star allele calling from SMRI SNP arrays using PharmCAT, generating continuous activity scores for 105 brains."
date: 2026-04-15
---

## Overview

Extracted pharmacogenomic variants from SMRI Stanley Array Collection SNP data and computed continuous CYP2D6/CYP2C19 activity scores using PharmCAT and established pharmacogenetic translation tables.

## Key Components

- **Input data**: SMRI Stanley Array Collection (n=105 postmortem brains, ~900K SNPs)
- **Variant extraction**: Isolated CYP2D6 (22q13.2), CYP2C19 (10q23.33) loci from SNP arrays
- **Star allele calling**: PharmCAT haplotype inference from genotype data
- **Activity scoring**: Continuous metabolizer scores (0.0-3.0 scale) instead of categorical bins

## Methods

Used PharmCAT v2.x pipeline for automated star allele calling from VCF-formatted SNP array data. Activity scores computed as sum of allele function values (e.g., *1/*1 = 2.0, *1/*4 = 1.0, *4/*4 = 0.0 for loss-of-function alleles). Continuous scoring preserves statistical power vs. traditional categorical metabolizer bins (PM/IM/EM/UM).

## Deliverables

- [PharmCAT pipeline script](https://github.com/codymgeoffroy/bioinformatics-2026/tree/main/projects/psychpgx/01_pgx)
- Activity score table (CSV): `pgx_activity_scores.csv`
- Star allele distribution plots (PNG)
- Quality control report: genotype call rates, allele frequency validation

## Results

*(Add figures when complete)*

- Distribution histogram of CYP2D6/CYP2C19 continuous scores
- Correlation plot between the two enzymes
- Comparison to published allele frequencies (validation check)
